backlog.title: Community Module Distribution (§18 Planned Migration)
backlog.description: Implement the §18 Community Module Distribution requirements (R1–R6) so the module can be consumed as a QMK Community Module instead of a keymap-local git submodule. This phase adds the module manifest, rewrites rules.mk to module context, adds an API-version assertion guard, rewrites README install instructions, and verifies no behavioral regression. The wire protocol (§4), the matcher (§7), the dispatch/OS/host-rules logic (§8/§14), and the notifier.h public API stay byte-for-byte unchanged (R6).

milestones.title: Module build-system plumbing (R1, R2, R3)
milestones.description: The three pieces that give the module its self-declaring QMK identity: the manifest (qmk_module.json), the module-context rules.mk, and the API-version assertion guard. After M1 the module is structurally consumable as a Community Module.

tasks.title: Add qmk_module.json manifest (R1)
tasks.description: Create the QMK Community Module manifest at the repo root with module_name, maintainer, license, url, and empty keycodes. No features block (RAW_ENABLE is not data-driven; §18.2 confirms no rawhid schema entry).

subtasks.title: Create qmk_module.json at repo root
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_76ea306f6be9/architecture/external_deps.md §qmk_module.json Schema — the schema requires only `module_name` and `maintainer`; `license`, `url`, `keycodes`, `features` are optional. Example modules in qmk_firmware (hello_world, super_alt_tab) both use `"license": "GPL-2.0-or-later"`. There is NO LICENSE file in this repo currently — QMK is GPLv2+, so GPL-2.0-or-later is the safe SPDX default.
2. INPUT: None — this is a new file at the repo root.
3. LOGIC: Per §18.3 R1, create `qmk_module.json` with: `module_name` = `"QMK Notifier"`, `maintainer` = `"dabstractor"`, `license` = `"GPL-2.0-or-later"` (GPL-compatible; matches QMK's own license and all example modules in qmk_firmware/modules/), `url` = `"https://github.com/dabstractor/qmk_notifier"`, `keycodes` = `[]`. Do NOT include a `features` block — the module declares no data-driven feature keys (`RAW_ENABLE` is set in rules.mk, not here; §18.2 confirms no `rawhid` schema entry exists). Do NOT include keycodes — the public surface is macros + functions invoked from keymap.c, not keymap-bindable keys.
4. OUTPUT: `qmk_module.json` present at repo root. Valid against the `qmk.community_module.v1` schema. If a QMK userspace build listing this module is available, `qmk lint` should be clean.
5. MOCKING: None — this is a static JSON manifest.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] The manifest fields are self-documenting. The `license` field must use the repo's real SPDX identifier — use `GPL-2.0-or-later` since there is no LICENSE file and QMK requires GPL compatibility.

tasks.title: Rewrite rules.mk to module context + retire submodule flow (R2)
tasks.description: Change rules.mk from the submodule-context form (SRC += qmk_notifier/notifier.c) to the bare module form (SRC += notifier.c). This retires the legacy submodule flow per the §18.4 'retire' decision.

subtasks.title: Change rules.mk to the module-context form
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_76ea306f6be9/architecture/external_deps.md §Community Module Generator — the generator auto-compiles only `{module_path}/{leaf}.c` via wildcard. Since `notifier.c` does NOT match the leaf name `qmk_notifier`, it is NOT auto-compiled and must be listed explicitly. `pattern_match.c` is pulled in by `notifier.c`'s `#include "pattern_match.c"` (resolved via VPATH, which build_keyboard.mk:536 confirms is on the compiler include path) — NOT a separate SRC entry. `RAW_ENABLE` is NOT a data-driven feature key (no `rawhid` entry in data/schemas/) so it MUST stay in rules.mk.
2. INPUT: Current `rules.mk` contains: `RAW_ENABLE = yes` and `SRC += qmk_notifier/notifier.c` (the submodule-context form with a keyboard-relative path).
3. LOGIC: Per §18.3 R2 and the §18.4 'retire submodule flow' decision, replace the SRC line: keep `RAW_ENABLE = yes` and change `SRC += qmk_notifier/notifier.c` to `SRC += notifier.c` (bare module form). The result is exactly two lines. This change is incompatible with the old `include keyboards/{...}/qmk_notifier/rules.mk` flow (the bare `notifier.c` is resolved via VPATH in module context, not via a keyboard-relative path) — this is intentional per §18.4.
4. OUTPUT: `rules.mk` is module-context. A module-listing keymap build compiles `notifier.c` (pulling in `pattern_match.c` via #include) and turns on `RAW_ENABLE` with NO manual rules.mk include line in the keymap.
5. MOCKING: None — static Makefile fragment.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a one-line comment in `rules.mk` noting this is the Community-Module context (`SRC += notifier.c` via VPATH) and that `pattern_match.c` comes in through `notifier.c`'s `#include`. Example: `# Community Module context — notifier.c via VPATH; pattern_match.c pulled in by notifier.c #include`.

tasks.title: Add API-version assertion guard to notifier.c (R3)
tasks.description: Add an #ifdef-gated ASSERT_COMMUNITY_MODULES_MIN_API_VERSION(1, 0, 0) guard immediately after #include QMK_KEYBOARD_H in notifier.c. The #ifdef ensures host/stub test binaries stay green (neither symbol is defined in the stub harness).

subtasks.title: Add the ASSERT_COMMUNITY_MODULES_MIN_API_VERSION guard
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_76ea306f6be9/architecture/external_deps.md §API Version Assertion — the generator (community_modules.py:252-254) defines both `COMMUNITY_MODULES_API_VERSION` and `ASSERT_COMMUNITY_MODULES_MIN_API_VERSION` as macros that expand to a `STATIC_ASSERT`. In the host stub harness (`-DQMK_KEYBOARD_H='"qmk_keyboard_stub.h"'`), neither symbol is defined (the stub doesn't pull in community_modules.h), so the `#ifdef` skips the assert entirely. notifier.c line 2 is currently `#include QMK_KEYBOARD_H`. The `STATIC_ASSERT` is a compile-time check, not a runtime one.
2. INPUT: notifier.c at current HEAD. Line 2 is `#include QMK_KEYBOARD_H`. The NFA_MAX_PATTERN override (#define NFA_MAX_PATTERN 128) is at lines 4-16, followed by `#include "pattern_match.c"` at line 18.
3. LOGIC: Per §18.3 R3, add immediately AFTER `#include QMK_KEYBOARD_H` (line 2) and BEFORE the NFA_MAX_PATTERN comment block:
```c
#ifdef COMMUNITY_MODULES_API_VERSION
ASSERT_COMMUNITY_MODULES_MIN_API_VERSION(1, 0, 0);
#endif
```
Target 1.0.0 because it is the floor that provides `housekeeping_task` and `process_detected_host_os` (confirmed in data/constants/module_hooks/1.0.0.hjson). In a real module build, QMK_KEYBOARD_H → quantum.h → community_modules.h defines both the macro and the assert, so it fires. In the host stub build, neither is defined so the #ifdef skips it.
4. OUTPUT: The guard is present in notifier.c. The three notifier stub binaries (dispatch / os / host) re-pass with 0 FAIL: lines unchanged — this is the direct proof that the #ifdef guard did not perturb the stub build. Run `./run_notifier_stub_tests.sh` to verify.
5. MOCKING: None — the #ifdef guard is self-isolating. In stub context it compiles to nothing.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a comment block on the guard: target version 1.0.0 (provides housekeeping_task + process_detected_host_os), why it is #ifdef-gated (host/stub tests define neither COMMUNITY_MODULES_API_VERSION nor ASSERT_COMMUNITY_MODULES_MIN_API_VERSION), and cite §18.3 R3. Example:
```c
/* Community Module API version guard (§18.3 R3).
 * In a real QMK module build, QMK_KEYBOARD_H → quantum.h → community_modules.h
 * defines both COMMUNITY_MODULES_API_VERSION and the assert macro, so this
 * STATIC_ASSERT fires and enforces API >= 1.0.0 (floor: housekeeping_task,
 * process_detected_host_os). In host/stub tests (-DQMK_KEYBOARD_H='...'),
 * neither symbol is defined so the #ifdef skips it harmlessly. */
```

milestones.title: Docs rewrite + verification (R4, R5, R6)
milestones.description: Rewrite README install/setup for the Community Module flow (the only user-facing change), add the leaf-directory naming contract, and verify no behavioral regression via the full host test gate.

tasks.title: Rewrite README install/setup for module flow + leaf-naming contract (R4, R5)
tasks.description: Replace the README 'Setup' section's submodule flow (git submodule into keyboard dir, relative #include, include rules.mk line, SRC/RAW_ENABLE hand-wiring) with the Community Module flow (clone into userspace modules/ dir, one keymap.json modules entry, no-relative #include, irreducible raw_hid_receive shim). Add the hyphen-free leaf-directory naming contract.

subtasks.title: Replace the submodule Setup flow with the Community Module flow
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_76ea306f6be9/architecture/system_context.md — the current README Setup section is at lines 49-98. It shows: (1) `git submodule add ... qmk_notifier` into the keyboard dir, (2) `#include "./qmk_notifier/notifier.h"` relative path in keymap.c, (3) `include keyboards/handwried/[manufacturer]/[keyboard_name]/qmk_notifier/rules.mk` in the keymap's rules.mk. The §18.2 hard limit confirms `raw_hid_receive` is NOT in the community-module hook surface — the user MUST still define it and call `hid_notify()`. The generator adds `VPATH += {module_path}` which is on the compiler include path (build_keyboard.mk:536), so `#include "notifier.h"` resolves without a relative path.
2. INPUT: The README's current Setup section (lines 49-98). All M1 artifacts are in place (qmk_module.json from P1.M1.T1.S1, module-context rules.mk from P1.M1.T2.S1, API guard from P1.M1.T3.S1).
3. LOGIC: Per §18.3 R5, replace the Setup section with the Community Module flow:
   - **Step 1 (clone):** Clone into the userspace at `modules/{org}/qmk_notifier`:
     ```bash
     cd /path/to/your/userspace
     git submodule add https://github.com/dabstractor/qmk_notifier.git modules/{org}/qmk_notifier
     ```
   - **Step 2 (keymap.json):** Add one entry: `"modules": ["{org}/qmk_notifier"]`
   - **Step 3 (keymap.c):** Include `notifier.h` with NO relative path (module dir is on -I via VPATH) and define the `raw_hid_receive → hid_notify` shim:
     ```c
     #include QMK_KEYBOARD_H
     #include "notifier.h"  /* module dir on -I via VPATH — no relative path */
     void raw_hid_receive(uint8_t *data, uint8_t length) { hid_notify(data, length); }
     ```
   - **Gone vs. today:** the in-keyboard clone, the `#include "./qmk_notifier/notifier.h"` relative path, the `include .../rules.mk` line, and the `SRC +=`/`RAW_ENABLE` hand-wiring.
   - **Still required (the §18.2 irreducible limit):** the `raw_hid_receive` shim — state this explicitly with a callout. `raw_hid_receive` is NOT a community-module hook, so the user must still define it.
   - **Multi-OS users** still set `OS_DETECTION_ENABLE = yes` and call `notifier_set_os` from `process_detected_host_os_kb` — unchanged from current README.
   - **Host-rules users** still define `DEFINE_HOST_CALLBACKS` — unchanged.
   - **Leaf-directory naming contract (R4):** Add a callout box or subsection stating explicitly that users MUST clone to a hyphen-free leaf (recommended `modules/{org}/qmk_notifier`). A hyphenated leaf is a HARD FAILURE — the generated `-DCOMMUNITY_MODULE_{LEAF}_ENABLE` define and `{leaf}` hooks would be invalid C tokens. This is the one setup mistake the README must call out prominently.
   - **Configurator note:** Add a brief note that Community Modules cannot be built by the QMK Configurator (this module requires a custom `raw_hid_receive` in keymap.c, which JSON keymaps cannot express, so Configurator was never an option; §18.4).
4. OUTPUT: README documents ONLY the module flow. A user can install with one `"modules"` entry + the `raw_hid_receive` shim. The leaf-name contract is stated. The old submodule flow, relative #include, and rules.mk include line are gone.
5. MOCKING: None.
6. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation task — it rewrites the user-facing install narrative (README.md Setup section) that spans the whole §18 delta. It depends on all of M1 (the flow documents what M1 lands) and is the single place the user-facing change is documented.

tasks.title: Verify no behavioral change: host gates green + R6 invariant (R6)
tasks.description: Re-run the full §11.2 host test gate with the §18 changes in place. Confirm the R3 #ifdef guard did not perturb the stub build. Confirm wire protocol, matcher, dispatch, and notifier.h API are byte-for-byte unchanged.

subtasks.title: Re-run the full §11.2 host gate; confirm module flow is byte-identical
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_76ea306f6be9/architecture/system_context.md §Baseline Test Gates — the baseline at HEAD is: run_all_tests.sh 2029/2029 assertions, run_notifier_stub_tests.sh dispatch=0/os=0/host=0 fails. QMK CLI 1.2.0 is available at /usr/bin/qmk, qmk_firmware at ~/projects/qmk_firmware, but there is no ~/qmk_userspace — a full module-build validation may need a userspace setup; if unavailable, fall back to qmk lint + host-test gates and mark the module-build criterion deferred.
2. INPUT: All §18 changes from M1 (qmk_module.json, module-context rules.mk, API guard) + M2.T1 (README rewrite) in place.
3. LOGIC: Per §18.3 R6 and §18.5, verify NO behavioral regression:
   1. Run `./run_all_tests.sh` → expect 9 pattern_match suites, 0 failures, perf sub-second.
   2. Run `./run_notifier_stub_tests.sh` → expect dispatch/os/host each 0 FAIL:, `✓ gate PASSED`. This is the DIRECT PROOF that the R3 #ifdef guard did not perturb the stub build — the load-bearing host-test-safety claim.
   3. If a QMK userspace + module-listing keymap is available: run `qmk compile`/`qmk lint` the module keymap; confirm the `0x81 0x9F` endpoint is live with NO manual rules.mk include line in the keymap, and that focus-change messages still switch layers / fire callbacks identically to today. If no userspace is available, mark this sub-criterion DEFERRED and rely on (1)+(2) plus `qmk lint` manifest validity (validate qmk_module.json against the community_module schema).
   4. Verify notifier.h, pattern_match.c, pattern_match.h are byte-for-byte unchanged (R6 invariant) — diff or hash check.
4. OUTPUT: Full §11.2 gate green with §18 changes in place. No regression. The module is structurally distributable and behaviorally identical.
5. MOCKING: None — running the existing test suites as-is.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] none — verification task, no user-facing documentation change.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final cross-cutting documentation sweep beyond the README Setup section: update any architecture overviews, test-count references, file-size tables, or feature lists that reference the submodule flow or omit the community-module capability.

subtasks.title: Sweep README and overview docs for stale submodule references
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The README Setup section rewrite (P1.M2.T1.S1) handles the primary install flow. However, other parts of the README or docs may still reference the submodule flow, the `SRC += qmk_notifier/notifier.c` path, or the `include .../rules.mk` pattern. The §17 file-size table may also need updating (rules.mk comment added, qmk_module.json is a new file).
2. INPUT: README.md after P1.M2.T1.S1. All §18 artifacts from M1.
3. LOGIC: Sweep README.md and any other docs for: (a) Stale references to the submodule flow outside the Setup section (e.g. in Troubleshooting, Contributing, or Compatibility sections). (b) References to `SRC += qmk_notifier/notifier.c` or `include keyboards/.../rules.mk` — update or remove. (c) The Companion Projects section — ensure it still references the correct naming convention (qmk_notifier firmware / qmk-notifier Rust crate). (d) File inventory — if README lists repo files, add `qmk_module.json`. (e) Any feature list or capability overview that should mention the Community Module distribution option. (f) The PRD §17 file-size table is authoritative in the PRD itself, not the README — no README change needed for line counts unless the README references them.
4. OUTPUT: README.md and any overview docs fully synced to the Community Module flow. No stale submodule references remain.
5. MOCKING: None.
6. DOCS: [Mode B] This is the final changeset-level documentation sync task. It depends on ALL implementing subtasks (M1 artifacts + README rewrite + verification) and sweeps any remaining cross-cutting doc surface that the P1.M2.T1 Setup rewrite did not cover.
